Title: A long noncoding RNA functions in high-light-induced anthocyanin accumulation in apple by activating ethylene synthesis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ethylene production and anthocyanin accumulation under high light by activating MdACO1 transcription
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdLNC610 is a high-light-induced lncRNA whose expression positively correlates with MdACO1 and anthocyanin accumulation. 2. Overexpression of MdLNC610 in apple fruit and calli increases MdACO1 expression, ethylene production, and anthocyanin accumulation, whereas silencing/RNAi has the opposite effect. 3. 1-MCP suppresses the anthocyanin-promoting effect of MdLNC610, while exogenous ethylene rescues coloration in MdLNC610-silenced fruit, showing an ethylene-dependent mechanism. 4. LUC, GUS transactivation, and Hi-C support that MdLNC610 activates MdACO1 transcription and is physically associated with the MdACO1 genomic region.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression and VIGS in apple fruit; stable overexpression and RNAi in apple calli; RT-qPCR; HPLC anthocyanin measurement; GC-MS ethylene measurement; promoter-GUS assay; dual-luciferase assay; Hi-C
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by high light in apple fruit peel
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly induced in debagged fruit peel under continuous high light; overexpression active in apple calli under high light
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High light (300 μmol m^-2 s^-1); moderate light (150 μmol m^-2 s^-1); 1-MCP; exogenous ethylene
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native MdLNC610 promoter in promoter::GUS assay
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq from previous transcriptome study, BioProject PRJNA555185 (SRX6464003-SRX6464008); Hi-C, BioProject PRJNA713418
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Proposed but not directly demonstrated; discussion suggests possible recruitment of chromatin/histone modification, phosphorylation, or DNA methylation-related factors to regulate MdACO1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance apple peel anthocyanin accumulation and coloration under high light or ethylene-related strategies; lncRNA-based engineering may improve fruit quality and ripening traits
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Physically located ~80,129 bp downstream of MdACO1 on chromosome 10; BOX4 light-responsive cis-element identified in the MdLNC610 promoter; mechanism interpreted as cis and/or trans activation of MdACO1

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes ACC to ethylene in the final step of ethylene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies upstream hormone signal that promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported in this study for high-light-induced ethylene production and anthocyanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by high light in apple fruit peel; positively associated with MdLNC610 expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in high-light-treated Red Fuji fruit and in overexpression calli; reduced by silencing/RNAi and by 1-MCP treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ethylene production and thereby enhances anthocyanin accumulation and red coloration under high light
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. High light strongly induces MdACO1 expression together with ethylene production and anthocyanin accumulation. 2. Transient overexpression of MdACO1 in apple fruit increases ethylene production, anthocyanin content, peel redness, and expression of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes. 3. Silencing or RNAi of MdACO1 reduces ethylene production and anthocyanin accumulation. 4. 1-MCP suppresses the MdACO1 overexpression phenotype, while exogenous ethylene rescues the silenced phenotype, demonstrating that MdACO1 acts through ethylene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression and VIGS in apple fruit; stable overexpression and RNAi in apple calli; RT-qPCR; HPLC anthocyanin assay; GC-MS ethylene measurement; pharmacological 1-MCP and ethylene treatments
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in some expression constructs; native MdACO1 promoter in LUC and GUS reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High light (300 μmol m^-2 s^-1); 1-MCP; exogenous ethylene
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq from previous transcriptome study, BioProject PRJNA555185 (SRX6464003-SRX6464008); Hi-C, BioProject PRJNA713418
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MdLNC610 (transcriptional activator relationship, not protein interaction)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene to enhance fruit coloration via ethylene-mediated anthocyanin accumulation, potentially combined with light management or MdLNC610 engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MdACO1 promoter lacked obvious light-responsive cis-elements, supporting indirect high-light induction via MdLNC610
